clusters.title: MarkItDown solves file-to-Markdown conversion more than...
clusters.summary: MarkItDown solves file-to-Markdown conversion more than clipboard semantic batching. It looks useful for PDF, DOCX, PPTX, and email imports, but it does not directly answer how Tino should judge routing or topic merge quality. Possible fit: optional import adapter for dropped documents later in the roadmap, not the hot clipboard path. Clipboard captures already arrive as plain text or rich text, so the first gap is HTML normalization and source attribution.
keyPoints: MarkItDown solves file-to-Markdown conversion more than clipboard semantic batching.
Possible fit: optional import adapter for dropped documents, not the hot clipboard path.
Pros: broad format coverage. Cons: Python dependency, file-oriented, not a quality pipeline by itself.
If we introduce it, keep it behind an import adapter boundary instead of coupling it to runtime evaluation.
clusters.reason: The batch repeatedly discusses runtime layering, review gating, and Rust-side persistence boundaries.

possibleTopics.reason: Tradeoffs among MarkItDown, HTML cleanup, rich-text stripping, and document-to-Markdown adapters for knowledge ingestion.
Offline fixtures, golden labels, replay runs, scorecards, holdout discipline, and regression tracking for AI quality.
Python used only for artifact analysis, statistics, clustering experiments, and notebooks after contracts are frozen.

keyPoints: Pros: broad format coverage. Cons: Python dependency, file-oriented, not a quality pipeline by itself.
If we introduce it, keep it behind an import adapter boundary instead of coupling it to runtime evaluation.
结论：对 clipboard 主链路帮助有限，对文档导入链路更有帮助。

promptText.system:
You are Tino's background batch compiler.
Return one JSON object only.
Do not use markdown fences.
Do not add explanation before or after the JSON.
The JSON must match this shape exactly:
{ "clusters": [{ "clusterId": string, "sourceIds": string[], "decision": "archive_to_topic" | "send_to_inbox" | "discard", "topicSlugSuggestion": string | null, "topicNameSuggestion": string | null, "title": string, "summary": string, "keyPoints": string[], "tags": string[], "confidence": number, "reason": string, "possibleTopics": [{ "topicSlug": string, "topicName": string, "reason": string | null }], "missingContext": string[] }] }

Important output rules
1. Output valid JSON only.
2. Use only sourceIds from the captures above.
3. Do not repeat sourceIds across clusters.
4. Assign every sourceId exactly once.
5. Use null when topic suggestion is unavailable.
6. Include missingContext and possibleTopics arrays even when empty.
